name: Greg Phillinganes
abstract: Gregory Arthur Phillinganes (born May 12, 1956) is an American keyboardist, vocalist, and arranger. A session musician, Phillinganes has contributed to numerous albums over a broad array of artists and genres.

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: I played in a really great band on the “Vibe” show with Greg Phillinganes
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: The drummer was Narada Michael Walden, and the — I can’t pronounce his name right, Greg Phillinganes was the piano player. Also Leon Pendarvis was a piano player... And it was an astounding rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: he had been in my band – well, he joined my band later. No, he did – he sat in with me and played some with me.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: So it was Greg Phillinganes, who is a wonderful synth keyboard player... I was playing another keyboard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003
